reviewer: retrospective critique backfill (code-grounded + existing conformance suite), revision 1

rubric.evidence: The demo runs a REAL in-browser image-classification pass: worker.js imports the shared loadPipeline (lib/webai.js) and calls the low-level forward (pipe.processor → pipe.model → logits) for the two-class ViTForImageClassification (sfw / nsfw), then softmax; also batch-screen and a downstream safety-gate helper. No canned result. Grounded in the actual worker source + the immutable conformance suite, which tests `loads-clean` (console-clean, headless Chrome) and `calls-real-inference` (source pattern: pipeline(|createModelLoader|...). id2label comes from pipe.model.config.id2label (defaulting to {0:'sfw',1:'nsfw'}); it is single-label softmax so the two probabilities sum to 1 — the 'safe to display?' verdict is a threshold on the real nsfw probability. NOTE: this is a retrospective code+suite-grounded score — no fresh measured latency/screenshot was captured in this backfill pass, hence 4 not 5.
rubric.notes: Inference runs off the main thread in a dedicated module Web Worker (new Worker(worker.js, {type:'module'})); scoreImage runs the forward + softmax; screen() batches many data-URLs; gate() answers a downstream safety question — all off-thread.

rubric.evidence: Expected output = a two-class {sfw, nsfw} probability + a clear 'safe to display?' verdict at your threshold. Shown as labelled probability bars + a verdict; See-inside lists raw logit + softmax per class. No canvas overlay.
rubric.notes: Defensive content-safety tooling: the image never leaves the device (private client-side screening). The verdict is an honest threshold on a real probability, not a black-box pass.

rubric.evidence: At-a-glance declares the exact model id (AdamCodd/vit-base-nsfw-detector, linked to its HF card), the real pipeline task (image-classification), backend (WASM — no WebGPU needed), quantisation (q8), download size (~88 MB) and license (Apache-2.0). The worker's loadPipeline call and the createModelLoader model config both use the SAME id/dtype/sizeMB — no invented API surface.

The page loads through the shared createModelLoader auto-init architecture (model:{modelId:'AdamCodd/vit-base-nsfw-detector', runtime:'transformers.js', dtype:'q8', sizeMB:88, requiresWebGPU:false}) — no hand-rolled Load button. A valid current on-device model auto-initialises (checking → initialising → ready); only Download/Re-download/Update surface otherwise. Conformance tests `shared-auto-init-loader` (source: createModelLoader).
rubric.notes: modelId keyed on AdamCodd/vit-base-nsfw-detector so the ~88 MB cache is tracked against the never-silently-re-download guarantee.

rubric.evidence: Loading/ready/inference state is announced in role=status aria-live regions (2 aria-live=polite regions in the page). Failures surface on the page as a readable `.status.err` message ("Screening failed: …"), never only in the console; the shared loader exposes Retry/re-download recovery. Runs on the WASM path everywhere — no WebGPU gate. Conformance tests `live-status-region`, `errors-on-the-page`.
rubric.notes: The screening loop surfaces per-item verdicts; a downstream gate() supports multi-model safety composition.

rubric.evidence: Real keyboard-operable controls: file dropzone (role=button), sample thumbnails (role=button, Enter/Space), a threshold range slider that moves the safe/unsafe boundary live, and a Screen-image button.

No canned/hardcoded output: every visible result is derived from the real worker run — the sfw/nsfw probabilities and verdict are computed from the real two-class logits. Conformance carries `no-fake-output` (manual-evidenced) and `calls-real-inference`.

Sample thumbnails carry alt text; the threshold slider and Screen button are labelled/named; status uses role=status aria-live; the verdict + bars are text DOM. Conformance tests `images-have-alt` and `buttons-have-names` (both script assertions over the DOM).
rubric.notes: models.json records support.desktop/mobile = needs-review with an automated pre-scan flag of several sub-24px tap targets (sample thumbnails / small chips) 'agent to verify tap size' — a genuine open item, not yet manually confirmed.

rubric.evidence: Layout uses the shared design system (public/styles.css) with fluid flex/grid, flex-wrap and intrinsic sizing; conformance carries `responsive-desktop` and `responsive-mobile` (no-horizontal-overflow at ≈1280×800 and ≈360×740 DPR3 touch) plus `parity-both-classes`.
rubric.notes: support.desktop/mobile are still needs-review in models.json (awaiting an agent parity verdict); models.json records support.desktop/mobile = needs-review with an automated pre-scan flag of several sub-24px tap targets (sample thumbnails / small chips) 'agent to verify tap size' — a genuine open item, not yet manually confirmed.

rubric.evidence: Inference runs off the main thread in a dedicated module Web Worker (new Worker(worker.js, {type:'module'})); The forward + softmax run in the worker; the threshold slider recomputes the verdict from cached probabilities without re-inference. No main-thread inference or unbounded main-thread loop — INP stays low. Conformance carries `inference-off-main-thread` (source) and `inp-stays-low`.

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible live region status role status aria-live; every control keyboard-operable with a name
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce loading/ready/error via role=status aria-live; give every control an accessible name; keep all controls keyboard-operable.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — role=status aria-live, labelled threshold slider + Screen button, text-labelled probability bars and a readable verdict.

guidanceConsulted.query: break up long tasks keep INP low off main thread web worker
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Break heavy synchronous processing out of the main thread (or yield) so the browser can handle input and repaint.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the classification forward + softmax (and batch screening) run in worker.js off the main thread.

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive layout no horizontal overflow fluid grid intrinsic sizing
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use fluid flex/grid with wrap + intrinsic sizing and logical properties; avoid fixed widths that force horizontal overflow.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — fluid flex/grid + design-system sizing; verdict + bars wrap.

openQuestions: A two-class NSFW ViT is a coarse safety signal (safe/unsafe only, no category breakdown) — is a multi-class content classifier worth an additive companion demo?
No fresh measured run captured here; a headless run citing a real sfw/nsfw score on a sample would let real-inference score 5. Sub-24px tap targets also need manual verification.

followUpGoals.goal: Capture a retained headless run citing a real sfw/nsfw score + verdict + latency so real-inference can be evidenced at 5.
Verify sub-24px tap targets and enlarge to ≥24px, then flip support to ok.

summary: Retrospective code+conformance-grounded critique for nsfw-image-detection (AdamCodd/vit-base-nsfw-detector, image-classification, q8, ~88 MB, Apache-2.0). Runs the real two-class ViT forward off-thread (single-label softmax, sfw/nsfw sum to 1) and turns it into a private, on-device 'safe to display?' upload gate with a live threshold slider, batch screening and a downstream safety-gate helper. Text-labelled bars + verdict (no opaque canvas), shared auto-init loader, live status + on-page errors. Honest gaps: coarse two-class signal, no fresh measured run captured here (real-inference 4), sub-24px tap-target flags at needs-review.
